author: Dr. Daniel Okafor / Dr. Marisol Vega
content:
MEDICINE ADMISSION NOTE (readmission)

SUBJECTIVE: Recurrent dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain, improving somewhat on oxygen and after starting the heparin infusion. Frustrated and frightened by the recurrence.

OBJECTIVE (data assembled; A&P intentionally brief):
- Recurrent/progressed PE — now bilateral with NEW right-ventricular strain (TTE) and NEW troponin elevation. Hemodynamically stable: BP maintained, normal lactate, on 4 L NC. Now INTERMEDIATE-RISK (submassive) PE.
- Key context: the recurrence occurred during a SUPERVISED 3-day peri-procedural HOLD of apixaban for the planned liver biopsy (see IR note). He was therapeutic on apixaban before the hold, with no recurrence in the interim.
- No contraindication to anticoagulation; now therapeutic on a heparin infusion.
- Liver biopsy cancelled today.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN (brief — see open questions):
#Intermediate-risk PE, recurrent/progressed in the setting of the anticoagulation interruption. Continue therapeutic anticoagulation (heparin infusion); monitor on telemetry; reassess RV status.
#OPEN ITEMS for the team: (1) the role, if any, of an IVC filter in this situation; (2) how to revise the biopsy/anticoagulation strategy so the malignancy workup can proceed safely.
#Hematology and IR to weigh in.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Daniel Okafor, MD (PGY-2) and Dr. Marisol Vega, MD. 8/10/2027.
